content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers LLL and R‚ÄÖ(L+3‚â§R)R\: (L+3\leq R)R(L+3‚â§R). Output any **four distinct** integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) such that their **bitwise `XOR`** is zero.

More formally, output any **four** integers a1,a2,a3,a4a_1, a_2, a_3, a_4a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,a3‚Äã,a4‚Äã such that:


a1‚äïa2‚äïa3‚äïa4=0a_1\oplus a_2\oplus a_3\oplus a_4 = 0a1‚Äã‚äïa2‚Äã‚äïa3‚Äã‚äïa4‚Äã=0
L‚â§a1,a2,a3,a4‚â§RL\leq a_1, a_2, a_3, a_4\leq RL‚â§a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,a3‚Äã,a4‚Äã‚â§R
ai=aja_i=a_jai‚Äã=aj‚Äã if and only if i=ji=ji=j
If more than one such quadruple exists, you may output **any** of them. If no such quadruple exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers L,RL, RL,R.


Output Format
For each testcase, output any **four distinct** integers between LLL and RRR such that their **bitwise `XOR`** is zero, or output ‚àí1-1‚àí1 in case no such quadruple of four distinct integers exists.
constraints: L+3‚â§R L+3\leq RL+3‚â§R, so there are at least four distinct integers in the range.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are only four integers in the range and their bitwise XOR is not zero. 1‚äï2‚äï3‚äï4=41\oplus 2 \oplus 3 \oplus 4 = 4 1‚äï2‚äï3‚äï4=4
Test case 222: There are many possible answers in this case. One of them is provided above: 3,6,9,123, 6, 9, 123,6,9,12. It can be verified that 3‚äï6‚äï9‚äï12=03\oplus 6 \oplus 9 \oplus 12 = 03‚äï6‚äï9‚äï12=0.
